texto: One day an intrepid sole will climb this mountain on its east side, reaching the summit and the passage that exist between the main peak and secondary peaks, by which he can descend to the west side of the mountain. It is at this site near Lake Brunner, between the main peak and an adjacent stone pyramid, in a "hidden cave" that has been sealed by earthquakes common in the region . . . where lust for Inca gold must end for some . . . but for that intrepid sole . . . it shall be just the beginning!
explicacao: Orientação direta de Steven J. Charbonneau sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “One day an intrepid sole will climb this mountain on its east side, reaching the summit and the p…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, adventure, discovery, útil em momentos de termino e discovery.
